content.feedback: leo update --source copies all memory/ docs from leo-core into the target project's memory/. This includes core decisions, feedbacks, and milestones that are specific to leo-core development — not relevant to downstream projects. The 24 core memory docs ended up mixed with each project's own docs.
content.how_to_apply: leo update should sync constraints/, skills/, schema.json, and identity.json from source. It should NOT copy memory/ docs from the source — those are the source project's knowledge, not universal core rules. Review computeSyncPlan to exclude memory/ from the sync scope.
content.why: User ran leo update on 6 repos. Each got 24 leo-core memory docs injected alongside their own project docs. Had to manually remove them. The update command should only sync constraints, skills, schema, and identity — not memory docs, which are project-specific by nature.
